changes.value:
In vim mode, typing `:` no longer opens the command palette, though `Cmd+Shift+P` still works fine.

The binding table has `":" -> "app.command"` and `normalizeKeyEvent` correctly produces `":"` for Shift+;. The global keydown handler at the document level should match this.

Suspected causes:
1. DOM focus may be landing in an editable element (EditableMarkdown title input) after clicking a card via FocusScope, and the input captures `:` before the global handler
2. The editable element may have a keydown handler with stopPropagation
3. The global handler doesn't skip regular inputs (only `.cm-editor`), so there may be a conflict

Files: `ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts`, `ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`, `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx`

- [ ] Investigate where DOM focus lands after clicking a card with FocusScope
- [ ] Check if EditableMarkdown or other editable components intercept single-character keydown events
- [ ] Add input/textarea/select skip logic to the global keydown handler for non-modifier bindings in vim mode
- [ ] Test: click card, then press `:` — palette should open
- [ ] Test: while editing a title, `:` should type normally (not open palette)
- [ ] Run tests to verify no regressions
Vim ':' keybinding no longer opens command palette

Suspected causes:
1. DOM focus may be landing in an editable element (EditableMarkdown title input) after clicking a card via FocusScope, and the input captures `:` before the global handler
2. The editable element may have a keydown handler with stopPropagation
3. The global handler doesn't skip regular inputs (only `.cm-editor`), so there may be a conflict
